overrides.override_summary: Covenant not to compete extending beyond FA term might not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: Post-term noncompete may be unenforceable; CA franchisee may be able to compete immediately after termination

overrides.override_summary: Litigation venue outside CA might not be enforceable for causes of action arising under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisee may not need to litigate in Illinois for CA law claims

overrides.override_summary: Application of non-California law might not be enforceable under California law
overrides.why_it_matters: California law may govern rather than Illinois law for CA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clause may be unenforceable under Civil Code §1671
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages provision (royalty x 24 months) may be voided

overrides.override_summary: General release as condition of renewal/transfer shall exclude claims arising under Hawaii Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: HI franchisee retains right to sue under HI franchise law even after signing renewal/transfer release

overrides.override_summary: Termination upon bankruptcy may not be enforceable under federal bankruptcy law
overrides.why_it_matters: Bankruptcy may not automatically terminate the franchise in HI

overrides.override_summary: Venue designation outside IL is void (but arbitration outside IL is permitted). IL Franchise Disclosure Act §4.
overrides.why_it_matters: Since franchisor is already in IL, this primarily protects IL franchisees from being forced to other venues

overrides.override_summary: No questionnaire or acknowledgement waives franchise law claims or disclaims reliance on franchisor statements. Supersedes any other term.
overrides.why_it_matters: Strong anti-waiver protection; franchisee retains all claims under IL franchise law

overrides.override_summary: Fee deferral: Initial franchise/development fees deferred until franchisor meets initial obligations and franchisee opens. Imposed by IL AG due to financial condition.
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchisee does not pay IFF upfront — payment deferred as financial protection. This is a material regulatory intervention reflecting the franchisor's weak financial condition.

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete limited to territory granted to franchisee per Indiana Code 23-2-2.7-1(9)
overrides.why_it_matters: Eliminates the 50-mile radius and nationwide scope; noncompete applies only within the franchisee's territory

overrides.override_summary: Franchisees permitted to commence litigation in Indiana for any cause of action under Indiana Law
overrides.why_it_matters: IN franchisee can sue in Indiana rather than traveling to Illinois

overrides.override_summary: Indiana law governs any cause of action arising under Indiana Disclosure Law or Deceptive Franchise Practices Act
overrides.why_it_matters: Indiana law controls over Illinois law for IN-specific franchise claims

overrides.override_summary: General release may not exclude claims subject to Indiana Deceptive Franchise Practices Law per Indiana Code 23-2-2.7-1(5)
overrides.why_it_matters: Release signed at renewal/transfer cannot waive Indiana franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: No general release shall exclude claims under Maryland Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisee retains all MD franchise law claims regardless of any release signed

overrides.override_summary: Litigation may be instituted in any court of competent jurisdiction including Maryland for MD law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchisee can sue in Maryland courts

overrides.override_summary: Maryland law shall prevail in the event of conflict if required by MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Maryland law may override Illinois law for MD-specific claims

overrides.override_summary: 90 days notice of termination (60 days to cure), 180 days notice of non-renewal per Minn. Stat. §80C.14
overrides.why_it_matters: Substantially longer notice/cure periods than FA provides, giving MN franchisee more time to respond

overrides.override_summary: No prospective general release of claims subject to Minnesota Franchise Law per Minn. Rule 2860.4400D
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisee retains all franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside Minnesota per Minn. Stat. §80C.21 and Minn. Rule 2860.4400J
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisee cannot be forced to litigate in Illinois

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete covenants generally unenforceable in North Dakota except in limited instances
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisee likely faces no post-term noncompete restriction

overrides.override_summary: No consent to termination or liquidated damages required from franchisees in North Dakota
overrides.why_it_matters: Liquidated damages provision may be void for ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: No general release excluding claims arising under North Dakota Law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisee retains all ND law claims

overrides.override_summary: Litigation venue outside ND is void for claims under ND Law
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisee can sue in ND

overrides.override_summary: North Dakota Law controls in event of conflict of laws
overrides.why_it_matters: ND law overrides Illinois law for ND-specific claims

overrides.override_summary: Forum selection/venue outside RI void for claims under RI Franchise Investment Act
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisee can litigate in RI

overrides.override_summary: General release void for claims under RI Franchise Investment Act
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisee retains all RI franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Development stage company risk warning. Unlawful for franchisor to use undue influence per §13.1-564 of VA Retail Franchising Act.
overrides.why_it_matters: VA explicitly warns of additional risk from development-stage company status and prohibits pressure tactics

overrides.override_summary: Initial franchise fee deferred until franchisor meets initial pre-opening obligations and franchisee opens
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisee does not pay IFF upfront — payment deferred as financial protection

overrides.override_summary: Washington Franchise Investment Protection Act prevails in conflicts of law
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law overrides Illinois law for WA-specific claims

overrides.override_summary: General release excludes rights under Washington FIPA
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisee retains all WA franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration must be in WA or mutually agreed location for franchises purchased in WA
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchisee need not travel to Illinois for arbitration

overrides.override_summary: Wisconsin Fair Dealership Law may affect termination provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: WI franchisee may have additional protections under fair dealership law
